questions.question: What is the primary function of the if statement in JavaScript?
options: A) To iterate over an array
B) To make a decision based on a condition
C) To call a function
D) To assign a value to a variable
questions.explanation: The if statement is used to make decisions based on conditions.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a while loop in JavaScript?
options: A) for (var i = 0; i < 5; i++)
B) while (i < 5) { console.log(i); i++; }
C) if (i < 5) { console.log(i); i++; }
D) switch (i) { case 0: console.log(0); break; }
questions.explanation: While loops continue to execute a block of code as long as the condition is true.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the switch statement in JavaScript?
options: A) To iterate over an array
B) To make a decision based on a value
C) To call a function
D) To assign a value to a variable
questions.explanation: The switch statement is used to make decisions based on the value of a variable.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a for loop in JavaScript?
options: A) while (i < 5) { console.log(i); i++; }
B) for (var i = 0; i < 5; i++) { console.log(i); }
C) if (i < 5) { console.log(i); i++; }
D) switch (i) { case 0: console.log(0); break; }
questions.explanation: For loops iterate over a block of code for a specified number of times.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the do-while loop in JavaScript?
options: A) To iterate over an array
B) To make a decision based on a condition
C) To call a function
D) To assign a value to a variable
questions.explanation: The do-while loop executes the block of code at least once before checking the condition.
